summary: FIDEM MVP end-to-end tested. Backend 26/26 passing after fixing a critical ObjectId serialization bug in /api/messages/send. Frontend mobile flow (signup -> 7-step onboarding -> candidates -> save -> profile -> chat -> nav tabs -> Me -> language switch -> Premium -> Admin) all verified.

minor.issue: On premium/vip grant, plan_until is set to now_utc() (current time, immediately expired) instead of a future date. No actual subscription duration is applied. Premium/VIP are effectively permanent because no expiry check exists, but the field is misleading.

Chat message inserted into db.messages mutates the dict with ObjectId. Not user-visible (not returned), but if any future code returns it, the same 500 bug as /messages/send will reproduce. Safe to .pop('_id') defensively.
No rate limiting; vulnerable to abuse/brute-force.

issues: Heading 'Welcome.' and CTA are in English even when language selector is set to UZ on first load — language toggle only affects post-login UI.
Top heading literally says 'Men' (Uzbek for 'I'), which can read like the English word 'Men' to non-Uzbek viewers; consider using a profile/avatar header or 'Profil' label.

action_items: Decide & implement actual subscription expiry: set plan_until = now + 30 days for premium/vip, and add an expiry check on /auth/me or a background job that downgrades expired plans.
Defensively pop('_id') after any db.collection.insert_one(doc) that may be serialized later (gift chat insert, photo_unlock replace_one).
Add basic rate limiting (slowapi or similar) for /auth/login and /auth/register.
Split /app/backend/server.py (1244 lines) into routers per domain (auth/profile/candidates/messages/payments/admin) — file size > 700-line guideline.
Optional: consolidate the two-step write in /profile/onboard and /profile (set fields + then set completeness) into one update_one call.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1244 lines — exceeds 700 line maintainability threshold; split into APIRouters.
apply_payment_success sets plan_until=now (no real expiry) — premium/VIP either never expire (no check) or are immediately 'expired' depending on intent. Business logic bug.
Two MongoDB writes in onboard/update_profile (set fields, then set completeness) — should be one $set.
candidate_can_message check for has_children uses 'is not None' which means filter=false will exclude users with has_children=true; intentional but needs explicit doc.
No pagination on /messages/chats, /messages/{chat_id} (limit 500). For active users this can grow unbounded.
CLICK callback skips signature when CLICK_SECRET_KEY is falsy — fine for sandbox but make sure prod env enforces it.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@fidem.uz / Admin@123. Test users created at runtime via /api/auth/register with random suffix (e.g. e2e_ui_<hex>@fidem.uz / Test@1234).
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 1 admin + 14 demo users on first startup (no extra seed needed for tests). Tests rely on these.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All major flows tested on mobile 414x896. Bug-fix applied: /api/messages/send was returning 500 due to ObjectId. After fix, all 26 backend tests pass and full frontend journey (register -> onboard -> candidates -> save -> profile -> chat -> me -> premium -> admin) works. If main agent adds new features, re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py first.
rca of the issue: Root cause: db.messages.insert_one(msg) in /api/messages/send mutates the input dict in place, adding the auto-generated ObjectId at key '_id'. The function then returned msg directly. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize bson.ObjectId, raising ValueError -> 500. Repro: any first authenticated message POST returned 500 (admin logs showed TypeError: "'ObjectId' object is not iterable"). Mitigation: msg.pop('_id', None) after insert_one before returning. Validated by re-running test_message_flow.
